body: Running 'gittoc update T-1' with no field flags still updates updated_at and commits with message 'fields updated'. This creates noise in the git history. The command should detect when no actual field change was requested (no --title, --body, --state, --owner, --label, --priority) and either no-op or warn. The underlying commit_if_needed already skips empty diffs, but the updated_at timestamp change creates a real diff every time.
title: update with no changes still commits
